title: Apple TV's 'Pluribus' Has a 100% on Rotten Tomatoes. Here's Why Critics Are Raving
text: Pluribus has received acclaim from critics, with praise for Vince Gilligan's writing and direction, Rhea Seehorn's performance, and the show's originality, tone, and stylistic influences. The show achieved notable critical success with a 98% approval rating on Rotten Tomatoes based on 182 critic reviews, while Metacritic gave the series a score of 87 out of 100 based on 38 critics, indicating "universal acclaim." Pluribus is officially the most-watched show in Apple TV's streaming service history. While critics were enthusiastic, audience reaction was more divided. Although "Pluribus" has been earning immense acclaim from many viewers and critics, some critics appeared focused on its incredibly slow pace, given how the show seems to have barely scratched the surface of its storytelling potential after seven episodes. Pluribus premiered on Apple TV on November 7, 2025, and a second season has been ordered.
